sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All green blocks are gone again. The board shows the blue region is now more connected. Let me check if there are any remaining green blocks and what the current state is. The level is still not completed, so I need to figure out what else to do.
Looking at the image, the blue region now extends from the top to the bottom, with the player on the right side. There's a black horizontal bar in the middle area. Let me check if there are any green blocks remaining and what the goal might be.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

greens = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green blocks remaining:", len(greens))

# Check all colors
all_colors = set(n['color'] for n in nodes)
print("All colors:", all_colors)

# Check blue regions
blue = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'S']
print(f"\nBlue regions: {len(blue)}")
for b in blue:
    print(f"  pixels={b['pixels']}")

# Check player position
player_b = next(n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b')
print(f"\nPlayer: {player_b['boundary']}")

# Check if level completed
print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Look at the layout
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nMiddle area (rows 25-40, cols 10-55):")
for i in range(25, 41):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][10:55]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
